contaminants.name: Arsenic
contaminants.description: Detected at 10% of FDA limit (0.01 mg/L). Naturally occurring in volcanic rock.

contaminants.name: Antimony
contaminants.description: Detected at 3% of FDA limit (0.006 mg/L). Can leach from PET bottles.

contaminants.name: Nitrate
contaminants.description: Detected at 2.5% of FDA limit (10 mg/L). Naturally occurring.

details.label: Source
details.value: Natural artesian aquifer, Viti Levu, Fiji Islands

sources.label: Fiji Water 2023 CA Water Quality Report

scores.notes: Scored based on Fiji Water's published 2023 CA Water Quality Report. Trace arsenic (0.001 mg/L) and antimony (0.0002 mg/L) detected, both well within FDA limits. 2024 voluntary recall for elevated manganese and bacteria in specific Nov 2023 production batches factored into contaminants score. No PFAS or microplastics testing disclosed. No third-party certifications (NSF, etc.) listed. Independent TrueRank testing pending.

minerals.description: Essential for bone structure, muscle function, nerve signaling, blood clotting.

minerals.name: Magnesium

minerals.name: Sodium

minerals.name: Potassium
minerals.description: Supports cardiovascular health, nerve function.

scores.notes: Scored based on Evian's published quality reports and NSF International testing. Strong mineral profile with notably high calcium (80 mg/L) and bicarbonate (360 mg/L). Very low sodium (6.5 mg/L). No contaminants reported. NSF International certified. Independent TrueRank testing pending.

details.label: Source
details.value: Natural spring, Ouachita Mountains, Hot Springs, Arkansas

details.label: History
details.value: Bottled since 1871, one of the oldest US bottled water brands

scores.notes: Scored based on Mountain Valley's published water analysis. No contaminants reported. Good mineral profile with high calcium (67 mg/L) and very low sodium (2.8 mg/L). Glass packaging eliminates PET leaching risk. No third-party certifications (NSF, etc.) found in public data. Independent TrueRank testing pending.

minerals.name: Calcium
minerals.description: Lower than most mineral waters, contributes to soft taste.

minerals.name: Magnesium
minerals.description: Minimal magnesium content.

minerals.name: Sodium
minerals.description: Very low sodium.

minerals.name: Potassium
minerals.description: Trace amounts.

details.label: Packaging
details.value: rPET (recycled PET), also available in Glass

details.label: Carbon Neutral
details.value: Certified CarbonNeutral for product and operations

scores.notes: Scored based on Icelandic Glacial's published water analysis and credentials. Exceptionally pure water with very low TDS (62 mg/L) and naturally alkaline pH (8.4). No fluoride, no contaminants reported. Low mineral content is a feature for taste but provides minimal nutritional benefit compared to mineral-rich waters. CarbonNeutral certified. Independent TrueRank testing pending.

minerals.name: Calcium
minerals.description: Exceptionally high. 35% of daily value per liter. Essential for bones, teeth, muscle function.

minerals.name: Magnesium
minerals.description: Very high. 26% of daily value per liter. Supports nerve, muscle, and enzymatic function.

minerals.name: Bicarbonate
minerals.description: Exceptionally high. Excellent natural buffer for digestive health.

minerals.name: Sodium
minerals.description: Moderate. Higher than still waters but natural for high-mineral sparkling.

minerals.name: Potassium
minerals.description: Good trace amounts for electrolyte balance.

details.label: Regulation
details.value: German Mineral and Table Water Ordinance (stricter than FDA)

scores.notes: Scored based on Gerolsteiner's published mineral analysis. Exceptional mineral content: 348 mg/L calcium (more than most calcium supplements) and 108 mg/L magnesium. Naturally carbonated from volcanic source. German Mineral Water Ordinance is stricter than FDA but no NSF or similar third-party certification. Higher sodium (118 mg/L) and very high TDS (2527 mg/L) may not suit all consumers. Independent TrueRank testing pending.
